sections.paragraph:
τρυγάω (cp. τρύγη ‘grain crop’) fut. τρυγήσω; 1 aor. ἐτρύγησα; aor. opt. pass. 3 sg. τρυγηθείη Job 15:33 (Hom. et al.; POxy 3313, 12f [II a.d.]; al. pap, LXX; JosAs 25:2) ‘gather in’ ripe fruit, esp. harvest (grapes) w. acc. of the fruit (POslo 21, 13 [71 a.d.]; Jos., Ant. 4, 227) Lk 6:44; Rv 14:18 (in imagery, as in the foll. places). τὸν τῆς ἀναστάσεως καρπὸν τρυγήσουσι 2 Cl 19:3. Cp. also the textually uncertain (s. αἱρέω 1) pass. Dg 12:8.—W. acc. of that which bears the fruit gather the fruit of the vine (cp. X., Oec. 19, 19; Diod S 3, 62, 7; Lucian, Catap. 20 τὰς ἀμπέλους τρ.; Philostrat., Her. 1, 2) or the vineyard (s. ἄμπελος a) Rv 14:19, w. ‘picking’ done through the use of a sickle, δρέπανον (JosAs 25:2; cp. Procop. Soph., Ep. 11 χωρία τρ.).—DELG. M-M.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

opt. opt. = optative

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

esp. esp. = especially

acc. acc. = accusative

POslo POslo = Papyri Osloenses—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

foll. foll. = followed, following

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

τρ.̠tr. = translate, etc.

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Her Her = Hermes, Zeitschrift für klassische Philologie—List 6

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1018
